Title: Elderly woman dies after van crashes into tree
Article:
JERTIH: An elderly woman was killed after the van she was travelling in skidded and crashed into a tree in Kampung Anak Ikan here about 1.50am today.
Besut police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahman said Munah Awang, 76, died while being treated at the Besut Hospital emergency unit about 4.30am.
"The victim was aboard a Toyota Hiace van with 10 other people, aged 12 to 60, who were on their way from Perak to Kampung Alor Serdang, Setiu.
"Preliminary investigations revealed that the driver lost control of the van, which was heading from Kota Baru, Kelantan towards Kuala Terengganu, before it skidded off the road and hit a tree," he said in a statement today.
Several other passengers who sustained minor injuries were also sent to Besut Hospital for treatment, he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan state assembly dissolution looms as MB crisis deepens, analysts warn
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The political crisis gripping Negri Sembilan could end in the dissolution of the state assembly if ongoing negotiations between Umno and Pakatan Harapan on the matter fail to produce a resolution.
Universiti Malaya political analyst Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said if talks broke down, the dissolution of the state assembly was the most likely outcome.
"If discussions fail, I am of the view that the Negri Sembilan assembly will be dissolved to break the current deadlock," he said.
He pointed to early signs that Pakatan Harapan was already preparing for that possibility, with an upcoming major ceramah attended by its central leadership set to be held in the state on May 6.
Tawfik predicted that Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun's time in office was running out regardless of how the crisis resolved.
He drew a parallel with the Selangor crisis of 2014, when Tan Sri Khalid Ibrahim was removed as menteri besar despite significant resistance before Datuk Seri Azmin Ali succeeded him.
University of Tasmania's Asia Institute professor, Dr James Chin, said any political settlement would ultimately have to satisfy the Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
"Whatever negotiations are done, they still cannot bypass the state ruler because at the end of the day, he will have a big say — he is the one who would be swearing in a new menteri besar," he said.
He said Umno's position was clear and did not extend to demanding a party change at the top.
Chin said what is known publicly is limited, saying matters touching on the Royal Institution in Malaysia is rarely fully transparent until well after the fact.
"Right now, I don't think anybody knows the complete picture," he said.
Yesterday, Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi had said the political standoff in Negri Sembilan is
"almost over"
.
He had said the matter would be addressed at a Barisan Nasional-Pakatan Harapan political bureau meeting next week.
This was as state Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias had said the 14 assemblymen from the party who had lost confidence in Aminuddin
will not be reversing
their position.
The situation in Negri Sembilan escalated after BN assemblymen withdrew support for Aminuddin, prompting speculation of a leadership change, which has since been ruled out at federal level.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had said Aminuddin would continue as menteri besar following a decree by the Yang di-Pertuan Besar.
In the 2023 state election, BN won 14 seats in the 36-seat assembly, while PH secured 17 and Perikatan Nasional five.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno adopts out-of-the-box methods to win over young voters, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno will adopt "out-of-the-box" approaches, including non-verbal communication methods, to win over young voters who now form the majority in the country's political landscape.
Its president, Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, said this reality must be acknowledged as voters below 40, including those under the Undi18 initiative, are more open-minded and no longer bound by party loyalty alone.
"They are very open in their thinking, and they do not necessarily choose parties. They choose candidates and those who can deliver agendas that matter to them, and what they stand to gain from the candidates they support," he said in a special interview in conjunction with Umno's 80th anniversary celebration here today.
Zahid said the strategy is crucial as voting trends have shifted, with voters increasingly assessing candidates based on service delivery and personal credibility rather than the political party they represent.
On the 16th General Election (GE16), Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, did not rule out contesting solo but remained open to cooperation to avoid overlapping seats with parties in the unity government coalition.
The deputy prime minister said the current political reality makes it difficult for any single party or coalition to emerge dominant and form a government on its own without support from others.
"That is why I mentioned the possibility of going solo. It does not mean we reject cooperation. We may still work together to avoid seat overlaps even if we contest on our own.
"In this matter, we are not rejecting any coalition partners," he said.
Reflecting on the hung Parliament situation following GE15, Zahid described the formation of the unity government as a reality that must be accepted in the interest of national stability, despite initial perceptions surrounding the cooperation.
"Such a situation may arise again, and I do not see any coalition in GE16 becoming dominant enough to form a government on its own.
"That is a reality we must acknowledge," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KLIA Terminal 2 balcony cordoned off after two foreign deaths
Article:
SEPANG: The balcony area on Level 3 at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 2, where two foreigners fell to their deaths, has been cordoned off from public access.
Police are investigating the two separate deaths that occurred on May 1.
The incidents —
the first involving a woman from China and the second an Algerian man
— are believed to be unrelated.
When the New Straits Times visited the Green Zone on Level 3, barriers had been erected around the section where the fall occurred, restricting public access.
Despite this, passenger movement in the busy terminal remained largely unaffected, with passengers and visitors continuing their routines.
However, the incident has quickly become a talking point among those passing through the area.
Several individuals were seen slowing down as they approached the barricaded section, exchanging hushed conversations and glancing towards the spot where the tragedy occurred.
Some lingered briefly near the barrier, discussing how the incident might have happened.
A security personnel stationed nearby said he only learned of the incident through a colleague.
"He told me it was quite scary," he said.
"I wasn't there during the incident. But this is the first time I've heard about someone falling to death in KLIA," he said.
"But people still need go about their business. It is truly shocking."
Another airport visitor, who frequently uses the area, said he was surprised to find it closed off.
"I only knew about the fall this morning after my brother told me. It is unfortunate that the incident happened," he said.
Meanwhile, KLIA staff declined to comment on the incident amid the ongoing investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Scams in Singapore: 20 Malaysians arrested in city-state since March
Article:
SINGAPORE: Twenty Malaysians have been arrested in Singapore since March for allegedly facilitating scam syndicates by collecting cash, gold, and valuables from victims.
In the latest case on April 29, a 21-year-old Malaysian woman was arrested for her suspected role as a money mule in an investment scam.
Police investigations revealed that a man was lured via a WhatsApp investment group and initially transferred SG$10,000.
He was then instructed to withdraw a further SG$50,000 to hand over to an "investment officer" at a mall in Ang Mo Kio.
The woman, believed to be acting as a money mule, was arrested on the spot after the cash handover was captured on closed-circuit television at the mall.
In a statement, the Singapore Police Force said that the woman is believed to have been directed by unknown suspects linked to a transnational syndicate to collect funds from victims and pass them along the chain.
She was charged with abetment by conspiracy to assist another in retaining benefits from criminal conduct.
The offence carries a jail term of up to 10 years, a fine of up to SG$500,000, or both.
The police said they are seeing a rising trend of Malaysians entering the country to assist scam syndicates, warning that tougher penalties now apply.
Offenders linked to scam operations face mandatory caning of at least six strokes, while money mules are liable to discretionary caning of up to 12 strokes.
A 23-year-old Malaysian man became the first money mule to be caned since harsher penalties for scam-related offences took effect on Dec 30, 2025.
He was convicted on April 29 of money laundering after collecting cash from an elderly investment scam victim in Singapore.
He was sentenced to seven months' jail and one stroke of the cane.
Court documents showed the accused was recruited via Telegram by an unknown person to retrieve funds from the victim, who had been duped into believing the money would be invested in a fictitious brokerage firm.
He collected SG$8,198 in Japanese Yen from the victim and handed it to another unidentified man.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police refute claim university student stabbed 105 times
Article:
KOTA BARU: Police today dismissed claims suggesting that a university student found murdered at a village in Ketereh on Friday succumbed to 105 stab wounds.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Azmir Damiri said investigations showed the 19-year-old victim died from 61 stab wounds.
Azmir said the results of the post-mortem did not include scratches and minor wounds.
"The post-mortem results conducted at Raja Perempuan Zainab II Hospital (HRPZ II) found 61 stab wounds on the victim's body ranging from the face to the feet, with most injuries concentrated on the right side of the body.
"It does not include scratches or minor wounds that were present, and we remain with 61 stab wounds," he said when contacted today.
Azmir was asked to comment on claims circulating on social media stating that there were 105 stab wounds on the victim's body instead of 61 as reported by the media.
It was previously reported that Nurfisya Zulkifly's body was
found by the public
at 12.30am in Kampung Simah, covered in blood after being stabbed.
The victim, who hailed from Kuala Kangsar, Perak, died due to multiple stab wounds to the chest.
Following that,
two 19-year-old men and an elderly married couple
were detained to assist in the investigation, with their remand periods lasting seven and three days, respectively.
Azmir said police are still identifying the motive for the murder and there have been no new arrests.
"The actual motive of the incident has not yet been identified, and investigations are ongoing for all suspects.
"So far, no new arrests have been made," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lee Lam Thye urges urgent action after Cheras child drowning
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye called for urgent measures, including installation of secure covers on drains, following the death of a nine-year-old girl in Cheras.
"We are deeply concerned by the recurring and tragic incidents of children falling into drains and being swept away, often during heavy rain.
"These are not isolated accidents — they are preventable tragedies that demand immediate and collective action," he told the New Straits Times.
The call comes after the girl who fell into a
drain in Cheras
on Friday was found dead today following a 3-day search along two rivers.
Lee said drowning remains one of the leading causes of death among children in Malaysia, with hundreds of lives lost each year, while urban drains and flood channels have increasingly become a hidden danger in residential areas.
He said such incidents pointed to gaps in infrastructure, planning and enforcement, and urged authorities to act decisively.
Among the measures proposed were nationwide safety audits of drains, particularly in residential areas, schools and playgrounds, as well as the installation of secure covers or grates over open drains.
He also called for barriers or guardrails along large monsoon drains, improved lighting and warning signage in high-risk areas, and stricter enforcement against contractors who fail to meet safety standards.
Lee said regular maintenance was important to ensure drain covers remain intact and safe.
Parents and guardians, he said, must remain vigilant, especially during rainy conditions, and ensure children do not play near drains, floodwaters or construction sites.
He also urged families to educate children on the dangers of fast-flowing water and to establish clear no-play zones in hazardous areas.
Communities, he added, should report damaged or uncovered drains and support local safety initiatives, while schools should include water safety awareness in their programmes.
Lee called for Malaysia to adopt a national drowning prevention strategy, including improved data collection, public education and safer urban design.
"No child should lose their life because a drain was left uncovered or unsafe.
"We urge all stakeholders — government agencies, local authorities, parents and communities — to act now before more innocent lives are lost."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid denies Tok Mat is mastermind of Negri Sembilan leadership crisis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan plays a key role in easing the Negri Sembilan leadership crisis, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
The Umno president said Mohamad, commonly known as Tok Mat, has consistently sought solutions through discussions with party leaders to defuse the situation.
As such, he said allegations linking Mohamad, who is also Rembau member of Parliament, as the mastermind behind the crisis in Negri Sembilan are unfair.
"Tok Mat Hasan is trying to find ways to resolve the issue. It is unfair to make such allegations against him. He frequently holds discussions and engages in discussions with me to find a solution.
"I also told him that the thread has become tangled, and we know the cause.
"As such, we must carefully and gradually untangle it," he said in an interview in conjunction with Umno's 80th anniversary here today.
Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional chairman, was responding to allegations by certain parties claiming that Mohamad was the mastermind behind the political turmoil in Negri Sembilan.
On Umno's stance regarding the state's administrative issue, Zahid said the decision by 14 assemblymen was to remain with the unity government.
"There is no question of withdrawing to become the opposition. That has never been an issue.
"There may be interpretations or perceptions that they disagreed with the appointment of the menteri besar, but this must be viewed in a broader context," he said.
The deputy prime minister said the appointment of the menteri besar is not decided by Umno alone, but depends on the consensus of coalition parties within the unity government, particularly Pakatan Harapan.
It was previously reported that 14 BN assemblymen
withdrew their support
for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
However, Zahid said on Thursday that all the assemblymen had agreed to continue supporting the state unity government to ensure stability.
Earlier on Tuesday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Aminuddin would continue to carry out his duties as menteri besar as usual after receiving the consent of the Yang Di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sri Lanka raises fuel prices as inflation spikes
Article:
COLOMBO: Sri Lanka raised fuel prices by nearly four per cent on Sunday, further fuelling inflation, which more than doubled last month due to the Middle East war.
Since March, Sri Lanka has raised fuel prices by more than 35 per cent, while gas and electricity rates have also increased by a similar amount.
The island has also rationed fuel following supply disruptions.
On Sunday, the state-owned Ceylon Petroleum Corporation increased the price of kerosene – used by agricultural machinery – to 265 rupees ($0.85) a litre, up 10 rupees.
Petrol rose 12 rupees to 410 rupees (US$1.32). Diesel was up 10 rupees to 392 rupees.
Higher energy prices pushed inflation to more than double, reaching 5.4 per cent in April, according to official data.
Fuel and electricity tariffs drove up transport costs as well as food prices, the Department of Census and Statistics said.
The island has been slowly emerging from the 2022 economic meltdown, when it ran out of foreign exchange reserves to pay for essential imports such as food, fuel and medicines.
However, it was hit hard in November by a cyclone that killed at least 643 people and affected more than 10 per cent of the island's 22 million population.
The storm caused an estimated US$4.1 billion in direct physical damage to buildings and agriculture, according to the World Bank.
The country has been stabilising its fragile economy with the help of a US$2.9 billion IMF bailout agreed in early 2023, but high energy prices have seriously challenged recovery efforts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Optimax set for earnings lift on MyAngkasa partnership, says CGS International
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Optimax Holdings Bhd is poised to see a meaningful uplift in earnings following its partnership with MyAngkasa Holdings Sdn Bhd, according to CGS International Securities Malaysia Sdn Bhd.
The firm said the memorandum of understanding, signed on March 9, 2026, enables Optimax to provide comprehensive eye care services to MyAngkasa's members and employees, marking its entry into a previously untapped public sector demographic.
With MyAngkasa's membership estimated at 7.5 million individuals as at 2025, CGS International views the collaboration as a "low-hanging fruit" for patient acquisition, supported by credit financing through Skim Pembiayaan Galakan Angkasa (SPGA).
"Our sensitivity analysis found that every 200 new patients, which implies a marginal market share of 0.01 per cent, can add RM900,000 net profit, which is equivalent to five per cent of its overall group net profit for financial year 2026 (FY26), all else being equal," it said in a note.
CGS International has raised its core earnings per share (EPS) forecasts for FY26 and FY27 by 6.8 per cent and 3.6 per cent, respectively, factoring in contributions from MyAngkasa patients, the ramp-up of existing ambulatory care centres (ACCs) and the rollout of ear, nose and throat (ENT) services expected to begin in the second quarter of 2026.
The firm also introduced its FY28 forecasts, projecting a net profit compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 16.5 per cent over FY25–FY28.
Growth is expected to be driven primarily by the maturing of key ACCs located in Atria, Kota Kinabalu and Cambodia.
It noted that these centres, which began operations between the second and third quarters of 2024, achieved pre-tax profit breakeven between the second and third quarters of 2025 and are anticipated to deliver full-year profit contributions from FY26 onwards.
Beyond FY26, CGS International expects Optimax's expansion into ENT services to significantly improve utilisation rates at its Atria ACC operating theatres, potentially increasing from around 40 per cent to about 70 per cent, as the facilities already meet Ministry of Health Malaysia requirements.
Additional growth catalysts include the planned opening of two new hospitals in Selgate and Kempas in FY26, as well as a regional expansion into Jakarta targeted for FY27.
CGS International maintained its "Add" recommendation on Optimax, with a slightly higher target price of 76 sen from 75 sen.
"We believe investor sentiment on Optimax should continue to recover as its earnings momentum strengthens," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two-thirds of proposed amendments to Mara Act focus on strengthening governance
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two-thirds of the proposed amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act focus on strengthening governance and improving administrative efficiency within the organisation.
Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the act, which has been in force since 1966 with only minor amendments, is no longer aligned with current governance needs and requires comprehensive reform.
He said the act centralises significant authority in the chairman and the Mara Council, a structure he said reflects outdated governance practices.
"In the 1960s, there was no concept of corporate governance. The concentration of power is very clearly placed in the chairman and the Mara Council.
"The existing framework exposes Mara to governance risks without proper checks and balances.
"In modern governance, there must be a clear separation between the board and management. This is why reforms and amendments are necessary," he said.
Earlier, at Mara's 60th anniversary celebration, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim directed that the draft amendments to the act be completed and submitted
within three months
.
"I hope the draft amendments to the Mara Act, which the deputy prime minister said would be completed by year-end, will not need to wait.
"These are all experts, all of them are skilled. Why do you want to wait for months? Give me the draft within three months," he said.
Asyraf Wajdi, when asked whether the review could be completed within three months, said the process is already in motion and does not start from zero.
"We have already begun and when I was entrusted as Mara chairman, the prime minister had already given a mandate that the most important matter to be looked into is Mara's corporate governance.
"(This is) because if we want to do anything, aspects of integrity, governance and financial management must be at the highest level... so that there will be no leakages, losses, wastage, or any form of misconduct.
"Moving forward, we must ensure that the Bumiputera agenda can be strengthened further without compromising integrity and good governance."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PublicInvest maintains an "Outperform" call on Avaland, keeping a 30 sen target price
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) has maintained its earnings forecasts for Avaland Bhd while reiterating its "Outperform" call on the property company with a target price of 30 sen, based on a roughly 60 per cent
The research house said Avaland's recent purchase of land in Taman U-Thant, Kuala Lumpur, is expected to bolster its position in the luxury residential market.
It noted that the acquisition will support Avaland's ongoing strategy to expand its footprint in the Klang Valley's high-end residential market, particularly in prime urban locations.
"This acquisition will further enhance the company's presence in the luxury residential segment, building on the strong market response and success of the group's earlier luxury developments such as Aetas Damansara (fully sold) and Aetas Seputeh (94 per cent sold)," it said in a note.
Avaland recently entered into a conditional sale and purchase agreement to acquire three adjoining parcels of freehold land measuring 0.76 hectares (ha) in Taman U-Thant, Kuala Lumpur, for RM86 million.
The land, acquired from Tong Ah Company Sdn Bhd, is priced at about RM1,050 per square foot and is earmarked for a high-rise residential development with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM700 million.
Funding for the deal will be sourced from a combination of internally generated funds and bank borrowings. Completion of the transaction is expected in the first quarter of 2027.
PublicInvest said Avaland's low net gearing of 0.24 times provides ample capacity to take on additional leverage.
The firm also pointed out that the land cost represents about 12 per cent of total GDV, which it views as attractive.
It said that this compares favourably with a recent transaction involving IOI Properties, where a 1.5 ha land sale to Paramount Corp Bhd was priced at around 23.4 per cent of GDV, or about RM1,600 per square foot.
PublicInvest also noted that Avaland is preparing for an accelerated launch pipeline in financial year 2026, with four new developments carrying a combined GDV of RM1.7 billion.
These include a second Aetas-branded luxury development in Kuala Lumpur, a high-rise mixed-use project in Petaling Jaya, Phase 2 of Anja Residences in Bangi, Phase 2 of Meria Commercial Hub and a new high-rise development in Cybersouth.
In FY25, Avaland launched RM670 million worth of projects, following RM1.8 billion in FY24. The group's landbank has also expanded with recent acquisitions in Petaling Jaya and Kuala Lumpur, adding about RM1.4 billion in potential GDV.
The company now holds a landbank of 75.7ha with an estimated GDV of RM11.6 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heavy rain, thunderstorms forecast in KL, Putrajaya and 11 states until 5pm today
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds for most states, including Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, and Putrajaya, until 5pm today.
In a statement today, MetMalaysia said the affected states include Perlis, Penang, and Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Yan, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu).
Similar weather conditions are expected in Perak (Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang, and Muallim).
Also expected to be affected are Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Kuantan, Pekan, and Rompin), Negri Sembilan (Jelebu and Seremban), and Johor (Segamat, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi, and Johor Baru).
Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun, and Kemaman) and Kelantan (Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Kota Bharu, Tanah Merah, Bachok, Machang, Pasir Puteh, and Kuala Krai) are also affected.
Meanwhile, in Sarawak, the areas involved are Miri (Subis and Miri) and Limbang, while in Sabah, the affected areas include the interior (Sipitang, Kuala Penyu, and Beaufort), the West Coast (Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu, Tuaran, and Kota Belud), Tawau, Sandakan, and Kudat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NCMP to boost solar adoption, green infrastructure investment: HLIB
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The National Carbon Market Policy (NCMP), which prioritises lower-cost abatement measures for domestic emissions reduction, is expected to accelerate momentum in solar energy adoption and energy efficiency retrofitting, according to Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB).
The research house also anticipates a pickup in investments across the waste management infrastructure segment.
The firm said that solar technology is already mature, scalable, and commercially viable, making it one of the earliest solutions likely to be adopted by corporates aiming to achieve voluntary environmental, social and governance and decarbonisation goals.
HLIB has kept its "overweight" call on the renewable energy sector, identifying Solarvest Holdings Bhd as a key beneficiary, underpinned by its 30 to 40 per cent share of large-scale solar projects and a strong order book pipeline.
"We understand the recent strength in renewable energy names has been driven by several supportive factors, including more stable solar module prices (US$0.11 to US$0.12 per watt), rising national focus on energy security, and the continued rollout of new renewable energy capacity schemes.
"We expect the NCMP to serve as a catalyst for gradually rising corporate demand for carbon reduction solutions and offset instruments," it said in a note.
HLIB said this is expected to benefit renewable energy players that can supply green electricity bundled with renewable energy certificates, supporting a strong long-term earnings outlook for the sector.
The firm said that postponing the carbon tax may ease near-term economic pressure, but it could also weaken the progress required for Malaysia to meet its ambitious climate targets.
It said the absence of a clear carbon pricing signal may reduce the urgency for high-emission industries to speed up investments in low-carbon technologies.
"Furthermore, prolonged delays could also weaken the competitiveness of Malaysian exporters, particularly those exposed to the European Union's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM).
"With the first quarter calendar year 2026 CBAM reference price at EUR 75.36 per tonne of carbon dioxide equivalent, exporters may eventually face carbon-related costs in overseas markets if equivalent domestic pricing mechanisms are not in place," it said.
HLIB said, in effect, a portion of the carbon value that could have been kept within the country may instead be transferred to foreign jurisdictions.
That said, the firm noted that if the strategic pause in implementing the carbon tax materialises, corporates could make productive use of the transition period.
"We believe companies that begin aligning internal emissions reporting with the NCMP monitoring, reporting and verification standards, while investing early in decarbonisation initiatives, will be better positioned to manage future compliance costs once carbon pricing measures are eventually introduced," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nestlé Malaysia outlook steady as cost easing offsets geopolitical risks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nestlé (M) Bhd's near-term earnings outlook remains steady despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, with analysts noting that operational resilience and easing input costs are helping to cushion potential risks.
In recent briefings, CGS International Securities Malaysia Sdn Bhd said the company's management remains confident that its diversified supply chain, easing cocoa and coffee prices and contingency measures will limit the impact of the conflict on earnings.
According to management, Nestlé's geographically diverse supplier base is expected to reduce exposure to supply-side disruptions.
The firm said that while lower cocoa and coffee prices, together with a stronger ringgit, are likely to offset higher fuel, logistics and other raw material costs through at least the second and third quarters of financial year 2026 (FY26).
However, Nestlé cautioned that if hostilities persist and cost pressures extend beyond the third quarter, price adjustments may be necessary.
It  also highlighted ongoing efforts to strengthen sustainability in its packaging, including increased use of recycled plastics and alternative materials, as well as diversification of resin sourcing to reduce supply risks.
The company added that prolonged high fertiliser prices could potentially pressure agricultural production into FY27.
Despite these challenges, CGS Internationl said that Nestlé's broad product portfolio across different price segments continues to support revenue resilience even if consumers reduce discretionary spending.
The research firm, however, trimmed its FY26 and FY27 core net profit forecasts by 1.6 per cent and 6.2 per cent, respectively, citing higher raw material and logistics costs.
It maintained a "Reduce" rating on Nestlé, with an unchanged target price of RM92, pointing to elevated valuations at 35.6 times FY27 earnings as a key concern.
CGS International also highlighted a shift in investor preference towards cyclical large-cap names as energy-related disruptions ease.
A projected 2.5 per cent FY26 dividend yield was described as insufficient to provide meaningful downside support.
Hong Leong Investment Bank (HLIB Research) took a more constructive stance, highlighting Nestlé's operational resilience amid geopolitical uncertainty.
It noted that about 20 per cent of raw materials transiting affected regions have been rerouted through alternative supply channels, while inventory levels have been deliberately increased to cover the next two quarters.
The research firm is also maintaining its "no price hike" stance for Q2–Q3, relying instead on efficiency gains to manage cost pressures.
HLIB Research added that easing cocoa and coffee prices are helping offset higher costs elsewhere in the supply chain, preserving gross margins.
On strategy, Nestlé is entering a lower capital expenditure phase, with spending expected to be roughly half of its previous RM1 billion three-year investment cycle.
Future investments will focus on maintenance, automation and digitalisation, alongside product innovation such as Nescafé coffee concentrates and Maggi air-fryer solutions.
HLIB Research maintained its "Buy" recommendation with an unchanged target price of RM135, citing attractive valuations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Uni student stabbed 61 times was kind-hearted, cheerful
Article:
KOTA BARU: A university student who was found murdered with 61 stab wounds in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, on Friday has been described as a kind-hearted and cheerful person.
A friend of the victim, who wished to be known only as Nurul, said that a day before the incident, they had gone out for breakfast with several other friends at a supermarket near their college in Lembah Sireh.
"Isya (Nurfisya Zulkifly) was her usual cheerful self. We chatted about many things during breakfast before heading back to our respective rented homes.
"I have known Isya for a long time, since our college orientation, and we have remained friends until now. I was incredibly shocked to hear the news of what happened to her," the 19-year-old said when met at Kolej Poly-Tech Mara (KPTM) today.
Nurul said that following the tragic incident, her parents and relatives advised her to take care of herself and be more cautious in her social interactions.
She said that since they rent accommodations off campus, they are now more cautious, always travelling in groups, and avoiding going out at night.
The management of KPTM has also issued a memorandum to all students, urging them to remain vigilant and always be accompanied by someone when travelling to prevent any untoward incidents.
KPTM is also holding a tahlil ceremony and Yasin recitation for Nurfisya at the college, and students living off-campus are invited to join the session via Google Meet.
It was reported that two youths who are suspects in the murder of the university student have been
remanded for seven days
.
Two other suspects, a 60-year-old woman and a 66-year-old man, were remanded for three days.
The case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, and whipping upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tasco FY26 earnings miss expectations on weaker cold supply chain segment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tasco Bhd reported financial year 2026 (FY26) core earnings that came in below expectations from both RHB Research and market consensus, largely weighed down by weaker performance in its cold supply chain (CSC) segment.
RHB Research said Tasco's FY26 core earnings were flattish year on year (YoY) at RM39.8 million, representing 90 per cent of the firm's and 102 per cent of consensus full-year forecasts.
The firm said this was mainly due to lower-than-expected contribution from the cold supply chain (CSC) arm, following the exit of F&B customers.
"Note that we adjusted the core earnings by RM2.3 million for FX impact, provision of doubtful debt, and write-offs," it said.
Looking ahead, RHB Research said Tasco should deliver positive earnings growth in FY27, driven by the commencement of its two new warehouses by mid-2026.
In addition, the firm said that Tasco stands to benefit from the higher freight rates arising from the Middle East conflict.
"Logistic players like Tasco are expected to benefit from higher freight rates, as fuel cost increases are typically passed through to end customers via fuel surcharges," it said.
However, RHB Research has trimmed its FY27 to FY29 earnings by 8.4 per cent, 10.4 per cent, and 10.5 per cent to factor in a lower contribution from the CSC arm.
Despite the revisions, the firm maintained a "Buy" call on the stock, though it lowered its target price to 61 sen from 70 sen, reflecting updated earnings assumptions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sombre mood as Nur Qaseh's family gathers after her body is found [WATCH]
Article:
KAJANG: A sombre atmosphere filled the Kajang Hospital forensic unit today as family members of Nur Qaseh Deandra Mohd Qayyum,9, gathered following the recovery of her body from Sungai Langat.
The child was found by search and rescue teams at 12.17pm, approximately 11km from where she reportedly fell into a drain in Bandar Mahkota Cheras on Friday.
At the forensic department, her parents and relatives waited in silence for the completion of the post-mortem. Health Ministry counsellors were also present to provide psychosocial support to the grieving family.
The girl's grandfather, Zulkarnain Ahmad, 57, told reporters the family was relieved the search had ended, despite the tragic outcome.
"I am deeply thankful because after three days of waiting without enough sleep or food, she was finally found. It is Allah's will, and we accept it.
"At least I now know where she is and where she will be laid to rest. Alhamdulillah, the family has come to accept what has happened," he said.
Recalling their last meeting, Zulkarnain said his granddaughter visited him about two weeks ago.
"She came to my house, and we ate together. She was very cheerful and made friends easily. She was very easy to talk to," he said.
He added that the loss was particularly difficult for the girl's siblings, especially her second-oldest brother.
"They were very close. If her brother did not want to go somewhere, she would not go either," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nearly 90 per cent of Kedah padi farmers yet to begin planting
Article:
ALOR STAR: Almost 90 per cent of rice farmers in Kedah, including those within the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) areas, have yet to begin planting for Season 1/2026 due to rising operational costs and a shortage in water supply.
Pertubuhan Persaudaraan Pesawah Malaysia (PeSAWAH) chairman Abdul Rashid Yob warned that the delay not only disrupts planting schedules but could also severely impact the country's Rice Self-Sufficiency Ratio (SSR) in the long term.
According to the Mada schedule, planting for Phase One should have commenced on April 15, followed by Phase Two on April 29 and Phase Three on May 13.
"The primary issue is the low dam water levels.
"The Muda Dam remains at a critical stage, and priority is currently being given to domestic use rather than irrigation for padi fields.
"Even if water were to be released in the near future, farmers still cannot afford to cultivate their fields because the costs have become prohibitively high," he said during a peaceful assembly of farmers at Kampung Alor Setoi here today.
Rashid said machinery costs have skyrocketed, with tractor rental rates increasing by up to 66 per cent, while harvester costs have risen by about 38 per cent.
Overall operational costs have surged by more than 50 per cent, placing an immense financial burden on farmers.
"This does not even include the cost of fertilisers and other inputs. With padi and rice prices remaining stagnant, farmers simply cannot bear this load.
"If padi prices increase, rice prices must also be adjusted, or the government must intervene to stabilise the situation," he said.
Despite recent rainfall over the past few days, Rashid said it has been insufficient to restore soil moisture or replenish the irrigation systems.
"We need significantly higher rainfall to ensure the canals have enough supply.
"Currently, conditions are still not conducive for planting even though we have entered May," he said.
He urged the government to take immediate action to address the pressing cost issues to safeguard the nation's food security.
"Just as we protect national sovereignty, we must also protect food sovereignty.
"Rice is our staple food, and farmers are the frontliners in ensuring its supply," he said.
According to the latest data from the Mada website today, the total combined water storage for the three dams under its supervision stands at 38.36 per cent.
The Pedu Dam recorded storage of 39.36 per cent, while the Ahning Dam stands at 51.31 per cent. However, the Muda Dam remains at a critical 8.24 per cent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two Gaza flotilla activists to appear before Israeli court
Article:
ASHKELON, Israel: Two foreign activists from a Gaza-bound flotilla who were brought to Israel for interrogation will appear in court on Sunday, an Israeli rights group said.
The flotilla of more than 50 vessels had set sail from France, Spain and Italy with the aim of breaking an Israeli blockade of Gaza and bringing supplies to the devastated Palestinian territory.
They were intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters off Greece early on Thursday, with Israel saying it had removed some 175 activists – two of whom were taken to Israel for questioning.
Spanish national Saif Abu Keshek and Brazilian Thiago Avilo are due to be presented before a court in Ashkelon on Sunday.
"So far they have not been brought before the court," Miriam Azem, international advocacy coordinator at the rights group Adalah, told AFP.
On Saturday, Adalah said its lawyers had met the two detained activists at Shikma Prison in Ashkelon.
Avila told the lawyers he had been "subjected to extreme brutality" when the vessels were seized, adding that he was "dragged face-down across the floor and beaten so severely that he passed out twice."
Since arriving in Israel, he said he had been "kept in isolation and blindfolded," according to Adalah.
Abu Keshek was also "hand-tied and blindfolded ... and forced to lie face-down on the floor from the moment of his seizure" until reaching Israel, the group said.
Israel's foreign ministry said the two activists were affiliated with an organisation that was sanctioned by the US Treasury.
That group – the Popular Conference for Palestinians Abroad (PCPA) – has been accused by Washington of "clandestinely acting on behalf of" Palestinian group Hamas.
Israel's foreign ministry said Abu Keshek was a leading member of the PCPA, and that Avila was also linked to the organisation and "suspected of illegal activity."
Spain has condemned the detention of Avila and rejected the Israeli accusation against him.
Organisers of the latest flotilla said the Israeli interception took place more than 1,000 kilometres from Gaza and their equipment was smashed, leaving them facing a "calculated death trap at sea."
Dozens of intercepted activists disembarked on Friday at the Greek island of Crete, according to an AFP journalist.
The Global Sumud Flotilla's first Mediterranean voyage to Gaza in the summer and autumn of 2025 drew worldwide attention, before Israeli forces intercepted the boats off the coasts of Egypt and Gaza in early October.
Crew members, including Swedish activist Greta Thunberg, were arrested and expelled by Israeli forces.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israel issues new evacuation warnings in southern Lebanon beyond occupied area
Article:
JERUSALEM: Israel's military on Sunday issued new evacuation warnings in southern Lebanon for villages beyond the area it occupies, despite a truce with Lebanon intended to halt fighting with Iran-backed Hizbollah.
"For your safety, you must evacuate your homes immediately and move away from the villages and towns by at least 1,000 metres into open areas," the military's Arabic-language spokesman Avichay Adraee posted on X.
The warning covers more than 10 villages and towns including several in the district of Nabatieh, which lies north of the Litani River, south of which Israel has stationed troops.
Lebanon's state-run National News Agency later reported a series of Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon, including on towns not mentioned in the evacuation warning.
Since April 17 a fragile ceasefire has been in place between Israel and Lebanon, which was aimed at pausing the violence between the Hizbollah group and Israel's military.
On Wednesday Israel's military chief of staff Eyal Zamir had threatened to strike Hizbollah "beyond the yellow line", which marks the area of Israeli control.
"Any threat, anywhere, against our communities or our forces – including beyond the Yellow Line and north of the Litani – will be eliminated," he said during a visit to Israeli troops.
In the past week two soldiers and an army contractor have been killed by drone attacks in the area, with dozens of soldiers wounded.
Hizbollah has recently begun using cheap drones controlled by fibre-optic cable, making them largely immune to electronic jamming, to conduct daily attacks.
These drones have a range of several dozen kilometres, which puts Israeli troops in Lebanon and communities in northern Israel under threat.
The United States has called for direct peace negotiations between Lebanon and Israel, but Lebanese President Joseph Aoun called on Wednesday for Israel to fully implement the ceasefire before the talks can take place.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mara vows safety overhaul after killing of 19-year-old student
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki has instructed Kolej Poly-Tech Mara Sdn Bhd (KPTMSB) to carry out a comprehensive review of student safety and welfare measures to prevent any recurrence of violent incidents, including murder, involving its students.
Asyraf said any shortcomings in safety and welfare must be examined in detail and improved to ensure student well-being.
"I have asked the management of Kolej Poly-Tech Mara to review student safety and welfare comprehensively.
"Any shortcomings must be scrutinised because I do not want the same thing to happen again," he told reporters on the sidelines of Mara's 60th anniversary celebration today.
Asyraf was commenting on the murder of a 19-year-old Kolej Poly-Tech Mara student, found dead with 61 stab wounds and left on a road in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, Kelantan on Thursday.
Mara has also arranged assistance for the victim's family, he said.
"We are offering assistance to the family in matters related to the victim.
"As for the rest, we leave it to the police, as this is now a criminal case," he said.
The body of Nurfisya Zulkifly was laid to rest at Kampung Periang Muslim Cemetery in Sauk, Kuala Kangsar, Perak yesterday.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said
4 suspects, aged between 19 and 66, were detained in two separate raids
at Taman Murni and Kampung Belukar Luas.
Police also seized two cars, two mobile phones, clothing, a pair of slippers, four floor mats, and a knife believed to be the murder weapon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asyraf Wajdi: Umno, PH to meet next week over Negri Sembilan crisis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno and Pakatan Harapan will address ongoing political tensions in Negri Sembilan through the unity government leadership consultative council meeting next week.
Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the situation in the state is related to party policies and the respective positions of both parties.
"So, I believe we should return to the agreement and understanding that formed the unity government.
"Within that framework, it is clearly stated that whenever there is any misunderstanding or dispute, we have our platform: the leadership consultative council, which will convene next week," he told reporters on the sidelines of Mara's 60th anniversary celebration today.
Asyraf Wajdi said one of Umno's political bureau resolutions recently was for both sides to engage and resolve matters relating to policy and party positions.
Meanwhile, asked if the meeting would also discuss the appointment of a new menteri besar, he said the matter was broader than any individual.
"There are many aspects that need to be examined, and that may be one of them.
"However, I can say that the issue in the state is not just about any individual. It is about institutions, specifically the sovereignty of the Royal Institution and adat (customs), which must be understood."
Yesterday, Negri Sembilan Umno said it
would rather sit as the opposition
than remain in the state government following its decision to withdraw support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
State Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias, on the sidelines of Umno's 80th anniversary celebrations, said the party did not want to be tainted by alleged wrongdoing involving the menteri besar in relation to the state's adat and constitutional crisis.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran plans to turn university site hit in US-Israeli strikes into museum
Article:
TEHERAN: Iranian authorities plan to turn a bombsite at a university in central Iran into a museum about the impact of US-Israeli strikes, an official said.
"The current damaged site will be preserved as a war museum at the university to remain a document of the country's scientific oppression in history," said Zafarollah Kalantari, head of Isfahan University of Technology on Saturday.
He added that other land had been allocated for the "construction of a new building and provision of advanced equipment" for the university, according to the official IRNA news agency.
Kalanari said initial assessments had put the cost of the damage to the university's buildings and facilities at around US$11 million.
US-Israeli strikes hit the institution in Isfahan – among the largest universities in Iran – in March during the war that began a month earlier and subsequently spread across the region.
Officials have said that strikes hit more than 30 universities across Iran, including in the capital Teheran, as well as residential areas and other civilian infrastructure.
A ceasefire has been in place since April 8.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 84-year-old man feared drowned in Sungai Petani river
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: An 84-year-old elderly man is feared to have drowned in a river at Kampung Masjid, Tikam Batu, today.
A fire and rescue team is currently conducting a search and rescue (SAR) operation at the scene.
Tikam Batu Fire and Rescue Station chief Assistant Superintendent Fauzi Shuaib said his team received a call at 8.56am.
A police report had earlier been lodged by the victim's family.
They believe the man had drowned after his personal belongings were found on the riverbank.
"The search operation is led by Zone 2 Officer of the Kedah Fire and Rescue Department, Fire Superintendent Bryan Del Bungkias, together with eight personnel from the Tikam Batu Fire and Rescue Station.
"The victim has been identified as Mohd Khalid @ Halim Salleh, 84.
"A six-member Water Rescue Team from the Kedah Fire and Rescue Department are at the scene conducting the search operation," Fauzi said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Queen departs for Uzbekistan to attend international Islamic civilization conference
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, has departed for Tashkent, Uzbekistan today to attend an international conference on Islamic civilisation.
The Queen will participate in the conference titled "Scientific Tradition in Islamic Civilisation: Sources, Potentials and Challenges", scheduled to take place at the Center of Islamic Civilisation in Tashkent.
Her Majesty, who is also Universiti Teknologi Malaysia chancellor, is set to deliver a royal address before more than 200 participants, comprising scholars, thinkers and statesmen.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir is expected to accompany the Raja Permaisuri Agong at the conference on May 5.
Earlier today, Her Majesty departed for Uzbekistan from the Royal Hangar at Senai International Airport.
Among those present to bid farewell and accompany Her Majesty were Istana Negara comptroller Tan Sri Dr Azmi Rohani and Johor Mufti Datuk Yahya Ahmad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysians warned over dubious 'badal haj' offers
Article:
JEDDAH: Malaysians are advised not to be fooled by dubious offers of badal haj (haj by proxy) services, especially those offered at "extraordinarily" low prices compared to the actual cost of the pilgrimage.
Malaysian Consul General in Jeddah Tengku Mohd Dzaraif Raja Abdul Kadir said the cost of performing haj is high, hence any unreasonable offer is feared to not meet the prescribed pillars and obligatory haj.
"If badal haj services are offered at a cost that is too cheap, we need to be careful because it may involve things that do not meet the requirements of the pilgrimage," he told reporters after the arrival of the first group of pilgrims at the Haj Terminal of King Abdul Aziz International Airport here today.
Earlier, a total of 276 pilgrims of group KT48 via the first direct KLIA-Jeddah flight arrived at 4.40am local time.
Mohd Dzaraif explained that for domestic haj packages in Saudi Arabia itself, the minimum cost is around RM9,000 to RM10,000.
"If there is an offer below this range, it may not be logical or is dubious," he said.
He added that so far no reports have been received involving Malaysians regarding badal haj scams, but cases reported by local media involving other countries prove that is still happening.
The public is advised to verify the details and not rush to make payments to any party offering the service.
Meanwhile, he reminded pilgrims of the strict enforcement by the Saudi Arabian government ahead of the haj season, including permit checks through the Nusuk system to ensure that only those with valid visas are allowed to be in Makkah.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC warns against sharing provocative 3R content online
Article:
CYBERJAYA: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission has issued a stern warning to the public against producing or distributing online content touching on race, religion, and the royal institution (3R), particularly material that is provocative, insulting, incites hatred, or risks triggering public tension.
"The MCMC takes seriously the misuse of network services and online platforms that could harm social harmony.
"The public is advised to verify the accuracy of information before sharing it, to avoid misunderstanding or hostility," the commission said in a statement today.
The commission said it would work with the police and relevant agencies to monitor, investigate, and take action against content that violated the law.
Offenders face action under Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act, which carries penalties of a fine of up to RM500,000, imprisonment of up to two years, or both.
MCMC also warned that it would take action against platform providers that failed to cooperate, reminding them of their obligation to comply with the law and to prevent the misuse of their services.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man dies 2 days after Johor crash, buried beside wife and daughter
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: A Proton Persona driver involved in a crash at Km71, Jalan Johor Baru–Mersing on Friday died last night.
He had earlier been reported in critical condition.
Mohd Aiman, 20, the third child of Kamilin Alias, 56, and Rubeta Mohd, 51, confirmed the death when contacted yesterday.
He said his father (Kamilin) was pronounced dead at 10.35pm.
On Friday
it was reported that 2 women were killed in a crash involving 2 cars on the Mersing Road
heading towards this district.
In the crash, Rubeta and Nur Isyirah Syakirah were pronounced dead at the scene, while Kamilin, who was seriously injured, was taken to Sultan Ismail Hospital in Johor Bahru for treatment.
Yesterday, the couple's second child, Mohd Izat, was reported as saying his father, who was initially conscious, was later sedated due to rapid breathing and underwent stomach surgery, with bleeding also reported in the lungs.
Kamilin's remains were transported by hearse from the hospital to Taman Sri Saujana mosque this morning for funeral prayers led by Mohd Izat.
He was later laid to rest at Makam Dagang Muslim Cemetery, Kampung Makam, beside his wife Rubeta and youngest child Nur Isyirah Syakirah, who were buried in a shared grave yesterday.
More than 100 family members, relatives and friends attended the burial to pay their last respects.
On Friday, district police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said authorities received information on the fatal crash involving a Proton Persona and a Perodua Bezza.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mental health at work to become legal duty for employers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) is moving to make mental health protection a statutory requirement for employers, framing psychosocial wellbeing as a legal duty under the Occupational Safety and Health Act.
The announcement was made in conjunction with the National-Level World Day for Safety and Health at Work 2026, officiated by Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan today.
DOSH said the new approach required workplaces to be adapted to meet both the physical and psychological needs of employees, with compliance expected across all sectors.
The push to strengthen mental health protection forms part of the Occupational Safety and Health Master Plan 2026–2030 (OSHMP30), which the department began implementing earlier this year.
Strategic Thrust 2 of the plan focuses on enhancing worker health and wellbeing, including mental health promotion, occupational health management, and the creation of workplaces that are safe from both physical and psychosocial hazards.
To support employers, DOSH has introduced the Guidelines on Psychosocial Risk Assessment and Management at the Workplace 2024 (PRisMA Guidelines), which provide a structured framework for identifying, assessing and managing psychosocial risks at work.
In the construction sector, DOSH is also enforcing the Occupational Safety and Health (Construction Work) (Design and Management) Regulations 2024 (CDM Regulations 2024), which adopt a life-cycle approach to risk management across all phases of construction projects.
The regulations assign clear responsibilities to all duty holders – including clients, designers and contractors – covering not only physical safety but also psychosocial factors that may contribute to workplace stress.
DOSH has also launched the CDM Regulations Guidelines 2026 Edition to further strengthen governance in the high-risk sector. It has also forged partnerships with Tun Razak Exchange City Sdn Bhd and IJM Corporation Berhad in developing OSHCDM compliance documentation under the new framework.
The event marked World Day for Safety and Health at Work, observed globally on April 28 under the International Labour Organisation.
This year's theme, "Together Ensuring a Healthy Psychosocial Work Environment", calls for collective action from the government, employers, workers and the wider community.
Jointly organised by the Human Resources Ministry, the National Council for Occupational Safety and Health, DOSH and the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso), the event also paid tribute to victims of workplace accidents and occupational diseases, while reinforcing efforts to build a stronger culture of prevention across industries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sarawak Water warns of low water pressure in Sibu after cable theft forced electricity shutdown at treatment plant
Article:
SIBU, May 3 — Sarawak Water Sdn Bhd (Central Region) has warned of possible low water pressure in several areas here following the theft of an earthing cable at the Salim Phase 1 Water Treatment Plant substation.
In an urgent notice posted on its Facebook page last night, the utility company said Sarawak Energy Berhad (SEB) will carry out a temporary electricity shutdown for about one to three hours from 11pm to facilitate immediate replacement works.
During the shutdown, the plant will operate using generator sets.
“This may result in low water pressure in certain areas, particularly during the shutdown period,” the utility company said.
Consumers are advised to store sufficient water and use it prudently while the works are underway.
Sarawak Water said supply is expected to recover gradually once electricity is fully restored and plant operations return to normal.
It added that all necessary measures are being taken to ensure the works are completed safely and in the shortest possible time.
The public is encouraged to obtain updates through Sarawak Water’s official communication channels.
Sarawak Water also apologised for the inconvenience caused and thanked consumers for their patience and cooperation. — The Borneo Post

Title: Man killed, four injured in two‑vehicle collision near Miri Hospital
Article:
MIRI, May 3 — A 43-year-old man was killed in a two-vehicle collision early today at a traffic light junction at Jalan Miri Bypass, near Miri Hospital.
Miri district police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the accident occurred when a four‑wheel‑drive (4WD) vehicle, driven by a man in his mid‑twenties, lost control while travelling from Permyjaya towards Taman Tunku and veered into the opposite lane.
He added that a car, driven by a 39-year-old man with three passengers, collided the 4WD after being unable to avoid the oncoming vehicle.
“The front passenger of the car was pronounced dead at the scene. The driver and two other passengers were sent to Miri Hospital for treatment. The 4WD driver also sustained injuries and was referred to Miri Hospital for treatment,” he said in a statement.
Mohd Farhan said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
He urged witnesses to contact the traffic investigating officer Insp Mohd Faizul Ab Rahim at 014-858 2582 / 085-430 479, or approach the nearest police station. — The Borneo Post

Title: Brace for evening thunderstorms: MetMalaysia warns of heavy downpours across the country
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a severe weather alert, warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds across 13 states until 7.00pm today.
Issued at 4.30pm, the short-term advisory cautions residents across the peninsula, as well as parts of Sabah and Sarawak, to brace for imminent heavy downpours.
In Peninsular Malaysia, the warning covers the entirety of Perlis, Penang, Perak, Negeri Sembilan, Melaka, and Johor.
Several districts in other states are also expected to be affected. In Selangor, the alert applies to Sabak Bernam, Klang, Kuala Langat, and Sepang.
In Kedah, affected areas include Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu.
On the east coast, the warning extends to Kelantan (Jeli, Tanah Merah, Kuala Krai, and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Dungun and Kemaman), and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera, and Rompin).
Across the South China Sea, the alert covers multiple divisions in Sarawak, including Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa, Betong, and Saratok), Sarikei (Sarikei and Meradong), Mukah, Bintulu (Tatau), Miri (Miri and Marudi), and Limbang.
In Sabah, the Interior, West Coast, and Tawau divisions, along with Kota Marudu in Kudat, are expected to experience similar weather conditions.
MetMalaysia issues these short-term warnings, which are valid for a period not exceeding six hours, whenever there are signs of a thunderstorm with a rainfall intensity exceeding 20mm per hour that is either imminent or expected to last for more than an hour.

Title: Umno to try new playbook for the under-40 crowd
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Umno will adopt ‘out-of-the-box’ approaches, including non-verbal communication methods, to win over young voters who now form the majority in the country’s political landscape.
Its president, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, said this reality must be acknowledged, as voters below 40, including those under the Undi18 initiative, are more open-minded and no longer bound by party loyalty alone.
“They are very open in their thinking, and they do not necessarily choose parties. They choose candidates and those who can deliver agendas that matter to them, and what they stand to gain from the candidates they support,” he said in a special interview in conjunction with Umno’s 80th anniversary celebration here today.
Ahmad Zahid said the strategy is crucial as voting trends have shifted, with voters increasingly assessing candidates based on service delivery and personal credibility rather than the political party they represent.
On the 16th General Election (GE16), Ahmad Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, did not rule out contesting solo but remained open to cooperation to avoid overlapping seats with parties in the Unity Government coalition.
The deputy prime minister said the current political reality makes it difficult for any single party or coalition to emerge dominant and form a government on its own without support from others.
“That is why I mentioned the possibility of going solo. It does not mean we reject cooperation. We may still work together to avoid seat overlaps even if we contest on our own. In this matter, we are not rejecting any coalition partners,” he said.
Reflecting on the hung Parliament situation following GE15, Ahmad Zahid described the formation of the Unity Government as a reality that must be accepted in the interest of national stability, despite initial perceptions surrounding the cooperation.
“Such a situation may arise again, and I do not see any coalition in GE16 becoming dominant enough to form a government on its own. That is a reality we must acknowledge,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Ahmad Zahid: ‘Unfair’ to accuse Tok Mat of masterminding Negeri Sembilan crisis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan plays a key role in easing the current administrative crisis in the Negeri Sembilan government, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
The Umno president said Mohamad, better known as Tok Mat, has consistently sought solutions through discussions with party leaders to defuse the situation.
As such, he said allegations linking Mohamad, who is also the member of parliament for Rembau, as the mastermind behind the crisis in Negeri Sembilan are unfair.
“Tok Mat Hasan is trying to find ways to resolve the issue. It is unfair to make such allegations against him. He frequently holds discussions and engages in discussions with me to find a solution.
“I also told him that ‘benang telah kusut’ (the thread has become tangled), and we know the cause. Therefore, we must carefully and gradually untangle it,” he said in an interview in conjunction with Umno’s 80th anniversary here today.
Ahmad Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, was responding to allegations by certain parties claiming that Mohamad was the mastermind behind the political turmoil in Negeri Sembilan.
On Umno’s stance regarding the state’s administrative issue, Ahmad Zahid said the decision by 14 assemblymen was to remain with the Unity Government.
“There is no question of withdrawing to become the opposition. That has never been an issue. There may be interpretations or perceptions that they disagreed with the appointment of the menteri besar, but this must be viewed in a broader context,” he said.
The deputy prime minister said the appointment of the menteri besar is not decided by Umno alone, but depends on the consensus of coalition parties within the Unity Government, particularly Pakatan Harapan (PH).
Previously, 14 BN assemblymen withdrew their support for the state government led by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
However, Ahmad Zahid said on Thursday that all the assemblymen had agreed to continue supporting the state Unity Government to ensure stability.
Earlier on Tuesday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Aminuddin would continue to carry out his duties as Menteri Besar as usual after receiving the consent of the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir. — Bernama

Title: Unity government council to discuss Negeri Sembilan MB row next week
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The Unity Government Leadership Consultative Council is scheduled to meet next week to discuss a range of current issues, including the status of the Negeri Sembilan menteri besar post.
Its secretariat chief, Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, said the meeting is important to deliberate on policy matters and positions among parties in the government, as well as issues involving the monarchy and customs in the state.
Asyraf, who is also Umno secretary-general, said discussions will be guided by the spirit of the memorandum of understanding (MoU) underpinning the formation of the Unity Government, which emphasises resolving differences through the consultative council.
“Insya-Allah, the meeting will be held next week and various aspects will be discussed comprehensively, and the matter (menteri besar’s position) may be among those discussed,” he told reporters after the Mara 60th anniversary celebration at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur here today.
Umno had earlier called for an urgent meeting between its Political Bureau and Pakatan Harapan (PH) to address the situation in Negeri Sembilan.
On Tuesday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun would continue to carry out his duties as menteri besar as consented to by the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir. — Bernama

Title: Fadhlina orders review of SPM Moral Studies after furore over ‘unfair’ grading
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek has ordered a full review of the SPM Moral Studies subject, stepping in after an outcry from parents and top-performing students whose university prospects are being jeopardised by unexpectedly poor grades in the paper.
Speaking at an event in Puchong today, Fadhlina acknowledged the “anxieties of parents and the community” and directed the Malaysian Examinations Syndicate to conduct the review.
She specifically thanked Sri Murugan Centre (SMC) director Surain Kanda for raising the issue,
Free Malaysia Today
reported.
“I have directed the Malaysian Examinations Syndicate to review and improve the subject,” she was quoted saying.
The review comes after reports that many high-achieving students received unexpectedly poor grades for the subject, potentially jeopardising their eligibility for matriculation programmes which often have stringent grade requirements.
According to Muda vice-president Siva Prakash, a former exam board manager, a key problem is the lack of transparency in how the paper is graded.
He explained that SPM grades are based on a moderation framework where grade boundaries are adjusted based on the student cohort’s performance, but these boundaries are not made public, leaving students and teachers in the dark.
At the same event, SMC’s Surain proposed that the Moral Studies exam should be an open paper using an “analytical marking” approach. He also criticised the grading threshold, calling the requirement to score 95 and above for an A+ “unreasonably high.”
Surain also urged the government to increase the number of matriculation placements for the Indian community to 2,500, without affecting the quotas for other communities.

Title: MCMC, police to act on provocative 3R material
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The public has been reminded not to produce or disseminate content related to race, religion and royalty (3R), particularly material that is provocative, insulting, incites hatred or could trigger public tension.
In a statement today, the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) said it takes seriously the misuse of network services and online platforms that could undermine social harmony.
"The public is advised to verify the authenticity of information before sharing it to avoid misunderstandings or hostility,” it said.
MCMC also said it will work closely with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and relevant agencies to monitor, investigate and take action against content that violates the law.
"Offenders may face action under Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, which carries a fine of up to RM500,000, imprisonment of up to two years, or both,” it said.
The commission stressed that action will also be taken against platform providers that fail to cooperate and reminded them to comply with the law and prevent misuse of their services. — Bernama

Title: Health Ministry says 56 heat-related illnesses, two deaths reported since January
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 3 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has recorded 56 cases related to hot weather nationwide, including two deaths from heat stroke, from Jan 1 to today.
MOH in a statement today announced that the two deaths involved a two-year-old boy who was left in a vehicle and a 42-year-old man who participated in a marathon in Penang.
“Both cases occurred when weather conditions were below Warning Level 1.
“However, the main risk factors identified were exposure to extreme heat in an indoor environment and strenuous physical activity over a long period of time, which caused severe dehydration and failure to regulate body temperature,” the statement said.
According to the statement, there were 47 cases involved heat exhaustion, four cases of exertional  heat stroke, four cases of heat stroke and one case of heat cramps.
MOH said analysis showed that 58 per cent of cases of heat-related illnesses were related to physical activity during hot weather.
“Of the total, almost half involved sports and athletic activities such as cross-country, sports training, and marathons, while the rest involved high-intensity physical training by security and operations teams.
“In this regard, MOH would like to advise the public to always be careful and take preventive measures during hot weather,” the statement said.
Among the preventive measures suggested by MOH are to limit strenuous physical activity and prolonged exposure to hot weather, and for those working under hot weather to spend more time resting in the shade to cool down.
In addition, the public is also reminded to drink plenty of plain water even if they do not feel thirsty to ensure that the body remains hydrated, as well as to use personal protection such as hats, umbrellas, light and brightly coloured clothing.
Risk groups such as infants, children, the elderly, and individuals with chronic diseases should always be adequately hydrated, and parents and guardians are reminded to avoid leaving children alone in vehicles.
The public is reminded to seek immediate treatment at a health facility if they experience symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, muscle cramps, or extreme fatigue.
“MOH also urges all parties including government agencies, educational institutions and care centres to take appropriate preventive measures, especially in planning and implementing outdoor activities to reduce the risk of heat-related illnesses.
“The public can get the latest information on the status of hot weather on the METMalaysia website and preventive measures to reduce the health effects of hot weather on the MOH website,” the statement said. — Bernama

Title: Missing Bandar Mahkota Cheras girl found dead in Sungai Langat after three-day search
Article:
KAJANG, May 3 — The body of a nine-year-old girl who fell into a drain and was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras two days ago was found in Sungai Langat near Jalan Reko here today.
Kajang Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief and operations commander Izman Adnan said Nur Qaseh Deandra Mohd Qayyum’s remains were discovered about 10.6 kilometres from where she was reported to have fallen.
“The victim was found by the search and rescue (SAR) team at about 12.17 pm, and her identity was confirmed by her parents at around 1.20 pm,” he told reporters here today.
He said the body has been handed over to the police and taken to the Forensic Medicine Department of Kajang Hospital.
Izman said rescue teams faced challenges accessing entry points to Sungai Long, but favourable weather conditions throughout the operation helped facilitate the movement of personnel.
The SAR operation, which entered its third day this morning, was expanded to cover a 29-kilometre radius, including Sungai Long and Sungai Langat towards Putrajaya.
On Friday, Nur Qaseh Deandra was swept away by strong currents while trying to retrieve a ball that had fallen into a drain while playing with friends near her apartment. — Bernama

Title: Anwar orders Mara Act amendments to be completed within three months
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed that the draft amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act be completed within three months.
Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the move includes streamlining Mara’s role as a key umbrella for the Bumiputera economy as well as a one-stop centre for development through better coordination of related agencies and policies.
He said emphasis would also be placed on governance to ensure the Bumiputera agency not only speaks up on key issues, but also demonstrates stronger commitment to good governance practices.
“In its 60 years, Mara has seen its ups and downs. There were times when governance issues were seen as weak and problematic. That is why I thank the Mara chairman for giving due attention to governance.
“I therefore expect the new Mara Act to be ready before the end of the year. I do not want to wait too long. There is no need to take seven to eight months…submit the draft to me within three months,” he said when speaking at the Mara 60th anniversary celebration at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur here today.
Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki and Mara director-general Datuk Zulfikri Osman.
The prime minister said the amendments are in line with one of the seven thrusts under the Mara 40-Year Vision, which is designed to position the institution as a key driver of a more inclusive, competitive and globally oriented Bumiputera economic development agenda.
He said the first thrust focuses on transforming Mara into a successful conglomerate anchored in education and entrepreneurship.
“The second emphasises the 3P approach - participation, ownership and control - to strengthen the economic foundation of the Bumiputera community.
“The third relates to talent development, including producing skilled and semi-skilled workers in line with evolving needs, particularly in sectors such as oil and gas and technologies like artificial intelligence (AI),” he said.
